semantic.role: Development build watcher script that polls source files and triggers rebuilds on changes.
responsibilities: Automatically rebuilds the ArchSpine project when source files change.
Polls specified directories and files for modification timestamps and sizes.
Queues builds if one is already in progress to avoid concurrent builds.
Reports build success or failure to the console.
outOfScope: Does not perform incremental compilation or hot module replacement.
Does not integrate with IDE or editor watch modes.
Does not clean the build output or handle complex dependency tracking.

English.purpose: This script provides a simple polling-based file watcher for the ArchSpine development process. It continuously monitors key source directories and configuration files, automatically invoking the build process whenever it detects a change. This reduces the need for manual rebuilds and speeds up the development feedback loop.
English.context_and_audience: Targeted at ArchSpine developers who need a lightweight, cross-platform method to automatically rebuild the project during active development. It avoids relying on system-specific file watchers and works reliably across different operating systems.
key_takeaways: The script polls every 1 second for changes in specified source files and directories.
It debounces builds by queuing requests if a build is already in progress.
The build is triggered via `npm run build`, and the console provides clear feedback on success or failure.
No external dependencies are required beyond Node.js and npm.
